summary.title: [Structured Data] Homepage publishes synthetic screenshot
summary.one_line_description: The homepage Organization JSON-LD still advertises the retired synthetic app mock instead of one of the verified Policy Advisor captures.

bug_1.file_locations: - demo/src/schemas/organization-schema.ts – `organizationSchema.image`, Line 22 - demo/src/app/page.tsx – `homeStructuredData` and homepage JSON-LD output, Lines 163-183

issue_description.description: The three visible homepage placements now load distinct verified Policy Advisor assets, but `organizationSchema.image` still points to `/img/screenshots/1.webp`, the retired synthetic mock that the change is intended to replace. `Page` includes that schema in `homeStructuredData` and serialises it into the rendered `script#structured-data`, so every homepage response continues to publish the stale visual to search-engine consumers even though the DOM images use the new `bulma-*` files. This leaves an externally consumed homepage image reference inconsistent with the visible product presentation and violates the requirement that no stale synthetic source references remain. Replace the Organization image URL with the approved representative Bulma capture and verify the rendered JSON-LD no longer contains `/img/screenshots/1.webp`.
issue_description.expected_vs_actual: | Aspect | Expected | Actual | |--------|----------|--------| | **Homepage image references** | Visible images and Organization JSON-LD reference approved current Policy Advisor captures | Visible images use `bulma-*` assets, but Organization JSON-LD references `1.webp` | | **External representation** | Search consumers receive a current, accurate Bulma application visual | Search consumers are directed to the retired synthetic mock | | **Old-reference removal** | No runtime homepage output contains the stale synthetic screenshot path | `script#structured-data` contains `https://bulma.com.au/img/screenshots/1.webp` |
